name: The Nuclear Question
discipline: Data Visualisation · Information Design · Interaction Design · Web Design · UX·UI
framing: A public platform that makes the case for, and against, nuclear energy legible to anyone.

blocks.body: The most ambitious thing I've built, and the bridge between my design practice and my thesis research. The Nuclear Question is a public-facing platform — live at thenuclearquestion.com — with interactive data-visualisation tools that make nuclear energy's strengths, weaknesses and risks navigable for non-technical audiences.

It examines the architecture of public communication around nuclear energy, and by extension other contested decarbonisation technologies: how framings of ownership, financing and risk travel from technical literature into mainstream UK discourse.

The work is grounded in original research — expert interviews with a nuclear-sector investor and a senior PR practitioner, and a public-perception survey (n=60) on misconceptions of risk, cost and waste — alongside an editorial data-poster series drawing on Our World in Data, the IPCC AR5, UNECE 2021 lifecycle estimates, Ember's UK generation data, and peer-reviewed energy-mortality figures.
It frames the designer as an epistemic facilitator, working in the tradition of investigative data journalism and climate accountability.
